ArchitectureNotes.objective: Enable reliable local-first web clipping from authenticated sites, producing offline-capable Obsidian notes with local attachments.
ArchitectureNotes.non-goal: No cloud backend, no multi-user collaboration, no mobile support in v1.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-1: Non-standard image loading on complex sites may reduce discovery accuracy.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-2: Large payloads may hit transport or memory limits before chunking is implemented.
ArchitectureNotes.risk-3: Upstream Web Clipper changes may require fork rebasing.

contract.purpose: Provide shared TypeScript types, Zod validation schemas, sanitization utilities, and constants used across all AuthClip packages

export-ClipErrorCode.PURPOSE: Union type of all error codes

export-AssetSource.PURPOSE: Asset origin: img-src | img-srcset | picture-srcset | css-bg

export-DiscoveredAsset.PURPOSE: Discovered image asset with id, url, source, selected

export-AttachmentPayload.PURPOSE: Binary attachment with metadata and base64 data

export-LinkMapEntry.PURPOSE: Maps original URL to attachment id

export-generateSafeName.PURPOSE: Generate unique filename with conflict resolution

export-buildResultStatus.PURPOSE: Derive overall status from attachment results

notes.note-1: Changes to shared types affect both extension and plugin — update both sides together.
sanitizeFilename, generateSafeName, extractFilenameFromUrl are used by both clipper-fork and obsidian-plugin.

contract.purpose: Render note naming and content templates with variable interpolation, 50+ filters, CSS selector resolution, and schema.org support

export-tokenize.PURPOSE: Convert template string into token stream

export-buildVariables.PURPOSE: Build variable map from page data

notes.note-1: Template engine is shared between clipper fork and upstream Web Clipper logic.
50+ filters in filters/ directory, each with co-located test file.
Variable resolvers: prompt, selector, selectorHtml, schema, meta, simple.

contract.purpose: Browser extension that discovers, fetches, and packages page assets from authenticated sessions, then sends to Obsidian plugin via HTTP

export-discoverAssets.PURPOSE: Find img[src], img[srcset], picture source[srcset] in DOM

export-parseSrcset.PURPOSE: Parse srcset attribute into URL array

export-sendCapturePackage.PURPOSE: POST package to Obsidian plugin HTTP endpoint

notes.note-1: Fork must not break standard Web Clipper clipping mode.
notes.note-2: All fetch operations use browser session cookies — no auth headers logged.
notes.note-3: asset-discovery-wrapper.ts is a background-script-compatible re-export of discovery + fetch logic.
notes.note-4: Transport: HTTP localhost, default port 27124, optional X-AuthClip-Token header.

contract.purpose: Obsidian desktop plugin that receives capture packages over HTTP, validates manifests, writes attachments, rewrites markdown, creates notes with frontmatter

export-validateCapturePackage.PURPOSE: Validate incoming package with Zod + version check

export-executeClipTransaction.PURPOSE: Full clip orchestration: paths → write → rewrite → note → report

export-writeAttachment.PURPOSE: Decode base64 and write binary to vault

export-rewriteMarkdown.PURPOSE: Replace external URLs with local wikilinks or relative paths

notes.note-1: Must validate incoming packages before any vault writes.
notes.note-2: Partial success: note saves even if some attachments fail.
notes.note-3: Dedup by SHA-256 hash is NOT yet implemented — name conflict resolution via generateSafeName only.
notes.note-4: HTTP server binds to 127.0.0.1 only; optional auth token via X-AuthClip-Token header.
notes.note-5: Frontmatter includes: source_title, source_url, captured_at, clipper_mode, author, description, published, site_name, domain, language, word_count, assets_saved, assets_failed.

DF-CLIP_FULL.NAME: Full Clip Flow
DF-CLIP_FULL.TRIGGER: User clicks Clip with local assets
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-1: Extension discovers image assets in page content (img[src], img[srcset], picture source[srcset])
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-2: User reviews and selects desired attachments in preview UI
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-3: Extension fetches selected assets in browser session context (credentials: include)
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-4: Extension assembles CapturePackage (markdown + attachments + linkMap + options + meta)
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-5: Extension sends CapturePackage to Obsidian plugin via HTTP POST /v1/capture
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-6: Plugin validates auth token (if configured) and JSON body
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-7: Plugin validates manifest with Zod schema + version check
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-8: Plugin resolves note path and attachment directory from settings
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-9: Plugin iterates attachments: check size, resolve unique path, write binary to vault
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-10: Plugin rewrites markdown URLs to local wikilinks or relative paths
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-11: Plugin prepends YAML frontmatter with source meta and asset counts
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-12: Plugin writes note file to vault
DF-CLIP_FULL.step-14: Extension displays success summary to user
DF-CLIP_FULL.evidence: V-M-CLIPPER-FORK discovery/fetch logs + V-M-OBSIDIAN-PLUGIN write/rewrite logs

DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.NAME: Partial Success Flow
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.TRIGGER: Some asset fetches fail during clip
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.step-1: Extension discovers and attempts to fetch all selected assets
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.step-2: Failed fetches are recorded with error code and redacted URL
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.step-3: Extension builds CapturePackage with successful attachments only
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.step-4: Plugin saves note with available attachments; lists failures in frontmatter
DF-CLIP_PARTIAL.evidence: FETCH_FAILED log markers + frontmatter assets_failed count

DF-DEDUP.NAME: Deduplication Flow
DF-DEDUP.step-1: Plugin lists existing files in attachment directory
DF-DEDUP.step-2: generateSafeName appends counter suffix to resolve name conflicts
DF-DEDUP.step-3: Future: SHA-256 hash dedup (Phase-4) will skip write when hash matches existing file

DF-PLUGIN_UNAVAILABLE.NAME: Plugin Unavailable Fallback
DF-PLUGIN_UNAVAILABLE.step-1: Extension detects plugin is unreachable after timeout (checkHealth)

Phase-1.name: Shared Protocol & Types
Phase-1.goal: Establish canonical types, schemas, and validation that both extension and plugin depend on
Phase-1.step-1: Define CapturePackage, AttachmentPayload, ResultReport, ClipSettings, error codes, sanitization utilities, and Zod schemas
Phase-1.step-2: Implement template engine with tokenizer, parser, renderer, 50+ filters, variable resolvers

Phase-2.name: Browser Capture MVP
Phase-2.goal: Implement asset discovery, authenticated fetch, and packaging in the browser extension
Phase-2.step-2: Implement asset fetch in browser session context (credentials: include)
Phase-2.step-3: Implement CapturePackage builder with linkMap and pathHint generation
Phase-2.step-4: Implement HTTP transport client with health check and auth token support

Phase-3.name: Obsidian Plugin MVP
Phase-3.goal: Implement HTTP receiver, manifest validation, attachment writer, markdown rewriter, and note creator
Phase-3.step-1: Implement HTTP server with /v1/health and /v1/capture endpoints, auth token validation
Phase-3.step-2: Implement manifest validator with Zod schema and version check
Phase-3.step-3: Implement attachment writer with base64 decoding and directory creation
Phase-3.step-4: Implement markdown rewriter (wikilink + relative modes) for markdown and HTML img tags
Phase-3.step-5: Implement note creator with YAML frontmatter (source meta + asset counts)
Phase-3.step-6: Implement settings UI with all configuration options
Phase-3.step-7: Implement path resolver (note path, attachment dir strategies, unique file names)
Phase-3.step-8: Implement VaultAdapter interface and ObsidianVaultAdapter implementation

Phase-4.name: Integration & Polish
Phase-4.goal: End-to-end integration, hash-based dedup, CSS background images, retry, security hardening
Phase-4.step-4: Implement partial success flow and enhanced error reporting
Phase-4.step-5: Security: token redaction audit, path traversal prevention verification, filename sanitization audit
Phase-4.step-6: Implement fallback "copy markdown only" when plugin unavailable

ExecutionPolicy.worker-owns: Only module source files and module-local tests inside the approved write scope.
